motivation: Extend K/V outlier asymmetry sample to Meta Llama family. Llama 3.1 8B is the primary mainstream 8B-class reference to compare with Qwen2-7B (catastrophic), Mistral-7B (safe), Qwen2.5-14B (safe), Phi-3-mini (safe), DeepSeek-7B (safe).

findings.cleanest_model_in_sample: Llama-3.1-8B has the lowest V_max (4.32x) across all 10 models measured. Meta's training recipe produces remarkably uniform activation distributions.
findings.late_layer_k_pattern: K_max 8.81x at Layer 29 (second to last) mirrors the Qwen2.5-14B pattern (10.65x at Layer 30 of 48). Both safe based on our 8x-threshold + late-layer-tolerance hypothesis.
findings.no_pathology: Layer 0 K = 3.09x is far below the 15x pathological threshold.
findings.comparison_with_qwen2_7b: Both use GQA but Llama's GQA is 32:8 (4:1 ratio) vs Qwen2-7B's 28:4 (7:1 ratio). The ratio itself does not appear to cause the pathology -- training recipe dominates.

pathology_rate_updated: 1 of 10 models (Qwen2-7B only). All other vendors (Meta, Google, Microsoft, Mistral, DeepSeek, Alibaba post-v2.5) exhibit controlled outlier patterns.
